subject: Biology
title: Freshwater Ecosystems
description: A fast breakdown of Freshwater Ecosystems for builders.

hook.text: Rivers, lakes, and wetlands hold less than 1% of Earth's water but support 10% of all species.

buildup.text: Freshwater ecosystems provide drinking water, irrigation, and food for billions of people.

discovery.text: Wetlands act as natural sponges—they absorb floods and filter pollutants from water.

twist.text: Freshwater species are declining twice as fast as land or ocean species worldwide.

climax.text: Dams, pollution, and water diversion threaten these ecosystems that humanity depends on.

punchline.text: One percent of the water. Ten percent of the life.

quiz.question: What natural function do wetlands perform?
